prerequisites.note: Frontend pages do not exist yet — the web app (apps/web) is a stub. This test plan is a specification for E2E tests once the frontend is built (expected in E6+ epics). E3.1 (Event Bus Infrastructure) has no API endpoints — it is internal infrastructure tested via backend unit tests only. E3.2 (Audit Trail) and E3.3 (Dead Letter Queue) expose admin API endpoints that will be surfaced through system admin UI screens. Tests assume a running API at :3000, a Vite dev server at :5173, a seeded database with test users, and Redis at :6379 for dead-letter queue functionality.

story_coverage.E3.1: No frontend test coverage — internal event bus infrastructure with no API endpoints or UI. Covered by backend unit tests (event-bus.test.ts, event-bus.plugin.test.ts).
story_coverage.E3.2: Journeys j01–j13 cover audit log viewing, filtering, entity history, pagination, multi-company isolation, and authorization. AC #1 is tested indirectly via event-triggered audit record creation. AC #2 is tested indirectly (immutability verified by backend). AC #3, #4, #6 are tested directly via UI queries.
story_coverage.E3.3: Journeys j14–j22 cover dead-letter queue viewing, filtering, reprocessing, error states, and authorization. AC #1 (retry) and AC #2 (DLQ persistence) are infrastructure-level, tested indirectly. AC #3, #4 tested directly. AC #5 (idempotency) is a development pattern, not UI-testable.

admin.description: Primary admin user with full access — has system.audit-log.list (view) and system.dead-letter-queue.list (view/edit) permissions

sales_staff.description: Limited user — does NOT have system.audit-log.list or system.dead-letter-queue.list permissions

finance_manager.description: Finance user — has system.audit-log.list (view) for compliance audit access

seeded_data.audit_log_records: Created by event bus during login and access group operations in E2/E2b stories
seeded_data.dead_letter_entries: Must be seeded by triggering event handler failures (or inserting directly into BullMQ for test setup)

journeys.name: Admin Login and Navigate to Audit Log
journeys.description: Verify that an ADMIN user with FULL_ACCESS can login and navigate to the Audit Log admin screen. The audit log page should display a filterable, paginated table of audit records scoped to the user's company.

steps.expected_result: Login page loads with email and password fields
steps.visual_check: Login form visible with email input, password input, and Sign In button
Dashboard loaded with app shell — sidebar visible with System module section
Audit Log page with filter controls (entity type, action, user, date range) and a data table showing audit records. Table columns: Timestamp, Entity Type, Entity ID, Action, User, AI Action

steps.expected_result: Table contains at least one audit record (from admin login event that was just generated)

journeys.name: Trigger Events and Verify Audit Records Created
journeys.description: Perform actions that emit business events (login, access group creation) and verify that corresponding audit records appear in the audit log. Tests the end-to-end flow: user action → event bus → audit service → audit_log table → query API → UI display.

steps.expected_result: Login succeeds — this emits a user.login event which the audit service captures

input_data.name: Audit Test Group
input_data.description: Group created to verify audit trail

steps.expected_result: Access group created successfully — this emits accessGroup.created event
steps.visual_check: Success toast or confirmation message visible

steps.expected_result: Audit Log page loads with latest records
At least one audit record with entityType 'AccessGroup' and action 'CREATE' is visible in the table
steps.visual_check: Audit log table shows a record with entityType=AccessGroup, action=CREATE, and the admin user's ID. Timestamp is recent.

steps.expected_result: At least one audit record with entityType 'User' and action 'LOGIN' is visible from the login performed earlier

journeys.name: View Audit Log with Default Parameters
journeys.description: Verify the audit log list view loads with default parameters: no filters, default limit (50), sorted by timestamp descending (most recent first).

steps.expected_result: Audit log page loads with default data
steps.visual_check: Audit log table populated with records. Columns visible: Timestamp, Entity Type, Entity ID, Action, User, AI Action. Records sorted by timestamp descending (newest first). Filter controls are in default/empty state.

steps.expected_result: Pagination indicator visible showing total count and current page
First record has the most recent timestamp (descending order)

journeys.name: Filter Audit Log by Entity Type
journeys.description: Apply the entityType filter to show only records for a specific entity type (e.g., 'User'). Verify the table updates to show only matching records.

steps.visual_check: Audit log table shows only records where Entity Type column reads 'User'. Total count in pagination reflects the filtered count. All visible records have entityType=User.

steps.target: All visible rows in Entity Type column
steps.expected_result: Every row in the table shows 'User' in the Entity Type column — no records of other entity types are visible

journeys.name: Filter Audit Log by Action Type
journeys.description: Apply the action filter to show only LOGIN actions. Verify only matching records are returned.

steps.visual_check: Audit log table shows only LOGIN action records. Action column reads 'LOGIN' for every visible row.

steps.target: All visible rows in Action column
steps.expected_result: Every row shows 'LOGIN' in the Action column

journeys.name: Filter Audit Log by Date Range
journeys.description: Apply dateFrom and dateTo filters to narrow audit records to a specific time window. Verify only records within the range are returned.

steps.expected_result: Date range filters set to today's date

steps.visual_check: Audit log table shows only records with timestamps from today (2026-02-21). All timestamps fall within the specified range.

steps.expected_result: All visible record timestamps are within the dateFrom-dateTo range

journeys.name: Filter Audit Log by User ID
journeys.description: Apply the userId filter to show only records for a specific user. Verify only that user's activity is returned.

steps.expected_result: User filter set to admin user's ID (or selected from a user picker dropdown)

steps.visual_check: Audit log table shows only records where the User column matches the admin user. All rows have the same userId.

journeys.name: Apply Multiple Filters Simultaneously
journeys.description: Apply entityType, action, and date range filters together. Verify the intersection of all filters is applied correctly.

steps.expected_result: Table refreshes showing only User/LOGIN records from today
steps.visual_check: All visible rows show entityType=User, action=LOGIN, and timestamps from today. Total count reflects the combined filter.

journeys.name: View Entity Change History
journeys.description: Navigate to the change history for a specific entity (e.g., an AccessGroup) to see its full chronological audit trail. Tests the GET /system/audit-log/:entityType/:entityId endpoint.

steps.target: View History link/button on an AccessGroup audit record row
steps.expected_result: Entity history page or panel loads showing all changes for this specific entity
steps.visual_check: Entity history view showing a chronological timeline of changes for the selected AccessGroup. Records are in ascending order (oldest first). Each entry shows: timestamp, action (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE), user who made the change, and before/after data diffs.

steps.expected_result: All records in the history belong to the same entityType and entityId. Records are ordered chronologically (ascending — oldest first).
First record should have action=CREATE (the entity was created before being modified)

journeys.name: Paginate Through Audit Log Records
journeys.description: Verify cursor-based pagination works: load first page, click next, verify second page loads with different records, navigate back.

steps.expected_result: Audit log page loads with first 5 records (assuming more than 5 total records exist)
steps.visual_check: Audit log table shows exactly 5 records. Pagination indicates 'hasMore: true'. Next Page button is enabled.

steps.expected_result: Second page loads with the next set of records
steps.visual_check: Table shows a different set of records. The first record on page 2 has an earlier timestamp than the last record on page 1. Previous Page button is now enabled.

steps.expected_result: Records on page 2 are different from page 1 — no duplicates across pages

journeys.name: Verify AI Action Fields in Audit Records
journeys.description: Verify that audit records display the isAiAction and aiConfidence fields. Currently all records should show isAiAction=false since no AI module is implemented yet.

steps.expected_result: Record detail view opens showing all fields
steps.visual_check: Detail view shows all audit fields including: isAiAction (false), aiConfidence (null or N/A), correlationId, beforeData, afterData as formatted JSON

steps.expected_result: isAiAction field shows 'false' or 'No' — no AI actions exist yet

journeys.name: Audit Log Rejects Unauthenticated Requests
journeys.description: Verify that accessing the audit log without authentication returns a 401 error.

steps.expected_result: Redirected to login page or shown 401 unauthorized error
steps.visual_check: Login page displayed (redirect) or error page showing 'Unauthorized' / '401' message. Audit log data is NOT visible.

journeys.name: Audit Log Rejects Users Without Permission
journeys.description: Verify that a user without the system.audit-log.list permission receives a 403 Forbidden response when trying to access the audit log.

steps.expected_result: Form fields populated with sales user credentials

steps.visual_check: Error page or toast showing 'Forbidden' / 'You do not have permission to access this resource'. Audit log data is NOT displayed. Sidebar may not even show the Audit Log link for this user.

journeys.name: Admin Navigates to Dead Letter Queue
journeys.description: Verify that an ADMIN user with FULL_ACCESS can navigate to the Dead Letter Queue admin screen and see the list of failed events.

steps.target: Dead Letter Queue link in sidebar (under System section)
steps.visual_check: Dead Letter Queue page with filter controls (Event Name, Reprocessed status) and a data table. Table columns: ID, Event Name, Error, Retry Count, Original Timestamp, Reprocessed, Actions. If no failed events exist, an empty state message is shown.

journeys.name: View Dead Letter Queue Entry Details
journeys.description: View the details of a failed event in the dead letter queue, including event name, payload, error message, retry count, and timestamp.

steps.expected_result: Dead Letter Queue page loads with entries (requires pre-seeded DLQ data)
Entry detail view opens showing full information
steps.visual_check: Detail panel shows: Event Name (e.g., 'accessGroup.created'), Payload (formatted JSON), Error message, Stack trace (collapsible), Retry Count (3), Original Timestamp, Created At, Reprocessed status (false). A 'Reprocess' action button is visible.

steps.expected_result: All required fields are present: eventName, payload, error, retryCount, originalTimestamp, reprocessed

journeys.name: Filter Dead Letter Queue by Event Name
journeys.description: Apply the eventName filter to show only DLQ entries for a specific event type.

steps.visual_check: DLQ table shows only entries where Event Name column reads 'accessGroup.created'. If no matching entries exist, empty state message shown.

journeys.name: Filter Dead Letter Queue by Reprocessed Status
journeys.description: Filter the DLQ to show only reprocessed or only pending entries.

steps.expected_result: Filter set to show only pending (not reprocessed) entries
Table refreshes showing only pending entries
steps.visual_check: DLQ table shows only entries where Reprocessed column reads 'false' or shows a 'Pending' badge. All entries have Reprocess action button available.

steps.expected_result: Filter changed to show only reprocessed entries
Table refreshes showing only reprocessed entries
steps.visual_check: DLQ table shows only entries where Reprocessed column reads 'true' or shows a 'Reprocessed' badge with reprocessedAt timestamp. Reprocess button is disabled or hidden for these entries.

journeys.name: Reprocess a Dead Letter Queue Entry
journeys.description: Trigger manual reprocessing of a failed event from the DLQ. The event should be re-emitted through the event bus and the entry marked as reprocessed.

steps.expected_result: Dead Letter Queue page loads with at least one pending entry

steps.target: Pending DLQ entry with Reprocess button
steps.expected_result: At least one DLQ entry has reprocessed=false and shows a Reprocess action button
steps.visual_check: DLQ entry visible with Reprocess button enabled. Entry shows event name, error details, and retry count of 3.

steps.target: Reprocess button on the first pending entry
steps.expected_result: Confirmation dialog appears asking to confirm reprocessing
steps.visual_check: Confirmation dialog: 'Are you sure you want to reprocess this event? The event will be re-emitted through the event bus for all subscribers.'
Success toast: 'Event reprocessed successfully'. The DLQ entry now shows reprocessed=true with a reprocessedAt timestamp. Reprocess button is disabled or hidden.

steps.expected_result: Entry shows reprocessed=true, reprocessedAt timestamp is set, and the Reprocess button is no longer available for this entry

journeys.name: Reprocess Already-Reprocessed Entry Returns 409
journeys.description: Verify that attempting to reprocess an entry that was already reprocessed returns a 409 Conflict error.

steps.expected_result: DLQ page loads filtered to show only reprocessed entries

steps.target: Reprocess button on an already-reprocessed entry (if UI allows it via API manipulation)
steps.visual_check: Error toast or inline message: 'Dead-letter entry has already been reprocessed' / 'Conflict'. The entry state remains unchanged.

journeys.name: Dead Letter Queue Rejects Unauthenticated Requests
journeys.description: Verify that accessing the dead letter queue without authentication returns a 401 error.

steps.expected_result: Redirected to login page or shown 401 unauthorized error
steps.visual_check: Login page displayed (redirect) or error page showing 'Unauthorized' / '401' message. DLQ data is NOT visible.

journeys.name: Dead Letter Queue Rejects Users Without Permission
journeys.description: Verify that a user without the system.dead-letter-queue.list permission receives a 403 Forbidden response.

steps.expected_result: Form fields populated with sales user credentials

steps.visual_check: Error page or toast showing 'Forbidden' / 'You do not have permission to access this resource'. DLQ data is NOT displayed.

journeys.name: Full Audit Lifecycle: Action → Audit Record → Entity History
journeys.description: End-to-end journey testing the complete audit lifecycle: perform an action (create and then update an access group), verify the audit records are generated, then view the entity's change history showing the CREATE and UPDATE sequence in chronological order.

steps.expected_result: Login succeeds — user.login event emitted → audit record created
Access group created — accessGroup.created event emitted → audit record (action=CREATE) generated
steps.visual_check: Success toast confirming access group creation

steps.target: Edit button on the newly created 'Lifecycle Test Group'
steps.expected_result: Edit form appears with current values
Access group updated — accessGroup.updated event emitted → audit record (action=UPDATE) generated
steps.visual_check: Success toast confirming access group update

steps.expected_result: Table shows only AccessGroup audit records
steps.visual_check: Audit log filtered to AccessGroup entities. At least two records visible for the 'LIFECYCLE_TEST' group: one with action=CREATE and one with action=UPDATE. Most recent (UPDATE) appears first.

steps.target: View History link on the LIFECYCLE_TEST group's audit record
steps.expected_result: Entity history loads for this specific AccessGroup
steps.visual_check: Entity history view shows two records in chronological order: 1) CREATE with afterData containing {code: 'LIFECYCLE_TEST', name: 'Lifecycle Test Group'}, 2) UPDATE showing the name change. Timestamps progress forward. Both records show the admin user as the actor.
